summary:
I compared the built change set against all 34 business requirements from the Define phase and emitted a **pass** verdict to `verdict.json`.

I traced each requirement to concrete evidence in the diff: the new `Phases::Pause`, `RerunMenuStep`, and `RestartDefine` services; the `Phases::Convergence` query; `ManagerTick`'s pause/restart settling logic; the `PhasesController` `pause`/`rerun_step`/`restart` actions plus routes; the additive widening of `Approve` and `AnswerQuestions`; the new `paused` status enum and the `pause_requested`/`restart_in_progress`/`restart_feedback` migration columns; and the paused-menu UI in `_define_panel`. Every requirement is satisfied — pausing that finishes the in-flight step then holds (R2/R3), a label-not-color paused state (R4), the exact five-item menu with inline fresh results (R7–R19), live "Done" settlement gating via `Convergence.phase_settled?` (R20/R21), stay-paused stepping (R22–R24), labeled failure recovery (R25/R26), overlap/conflict guards backed by the unique step-run index (R29/R30), and untouched existing gate/rework/answer paths (R31–R34). I noted one nuance on R28 (manual re-runs share the per-step iteration counter the auto cap reads) but confirmed it only interacts during a Repeat-from-the-Beginning restart — an explicitly documented, accepted design decision — so R28's core intent holds and it is not a deviation. Findings are empty. I could not execute the test suite (gems aren't installed in this worktree), but the assessment is based on reading the implementation, and the build's own test-critic step already passed.
